title: alchemilla vulgaris var. racemulosa
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris var. racemulosa, also known as Lady's Mantle, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its rosette of leaves and clusters of small, greenish-yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and protect against infections. The health benefits of Lady's Mantle include its ability to regulate menstrual cycles, reduce menstrual cramps, and alleviate symptoms of menopause, as well as its potential to reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Lady's Mantle is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used topically as a salve or cream to treat skin conditions and promote wound healing.
